regime_events.description: Google Bidding lost for US PS — primary driver of -25% US reg decline vs 2024 baseline through May 2025 when OCI launched. Full impact visible July 2025. Walmart Business brand competition emerged Q4 2024, further +40% Brand CPA impact. [ADDED 2026-04-22.]

Walmart Business Brand competition emerges, showing on >30% of "Amazon Business" branded searches. Drove US Brand CPA from $44 (Y24) to $77 (Y25) YoY, +40% CPC. Persistent structural baseline for US Brand economics. 2024 Q4 onwards. [ADDED 2026-04-22 from 2025 MBR regime audit.]

US Guest experience PS test launched 2025-01-22, paused 2025-02-12 due to reduced registrations (SHUMA-ineligible traffic issue + Weblab calc mismatch). Weeks 4-7 of 2025 excluded from US elasticity fit. Restarted 2025-03-13 after fixes. [ADDED 2026-04-22.]
US OCI Brand+NB complete
Promo launch causing CPC spikes on music keywords

regime_fit_state.description: Google Bidding lost for US PS — primary driver of -25% US reg decline vs 2024 baseline through May 2025 when OCI launched. Full impact visible July 2025. Walmart Business brand competition emerged Q4 2024, further +40% Brand CPA impact. [ADDED 2026-04-22.]

Walmart Business Brand competition emerges, showing on >30% of "Amazon Business" branded searches. Drove US Brand CPA from $44 (Y24) to $77 (Y25) YoY, +40% CPC. Persistent structural baseline for US Brand economics. 2024 Q4 onwards. [ADDED 2026-04-22 from 2025 MBR regime audit.]
US OCI Brand+NB complete

value_json.description: Current run-rate projection with no adjustments
12% Brand uplift decaying over 12 weeks (novelty effect)

value_json.template:
MX {time_period} projection: {total_regs:,.0f} registrations on {total_spend_fmt} spend, blended CPA {blended_cpa_fmt}, ie%CCP {ieccp:.1f}% vs 100% target. 90% credible interval on total regs: {regs_ci90_lo:,.0f} to {regs_ci90_hi:,.0f}. Brand contributes {brand_regs:,.0f} regs ({brand_pct:.0f}% of spend); NB contributes {nb_regs:,.0f} regs.

MX is an ie%CCP-bound market — Lorena manages against the 100% ceiling, which means every spend lever is evaluated against whether it moves us closer to 100% or further away. Current baseline reflects Polaris INTL post-launch (2025-08-28 structural shift), with Semana Santa and 2026-W15 NB drop accounted for as transients with known decay profiles.

Fit quality: Brand CPA elasticity r²=0.56 (market-specific, 110 weeks) but 12-week holdout MAPE 64.7% — holdout spans three regime events (Polaris + Semana Santa + W15). NB CPA elasticity r²=0.59 with holdout MAPE 13.3% (clean). YoY is a growth-ramp artifact (+69% ±114%) — multi-year MX projections fire VERY_WIDE_CI per R11.8.

For Lorena conversations: Brand is the demand-clean lever (11% of spend but ~3.5× the CCP impact per reg). NB is the volume lever. The W15 NB drop investigation with Yun-Kang is live; expect provisional regime flag to resolve at first refit (2026-07-15).

regime_events.description: MX ie%CCP gate flip: ungated -> 100% ceiling enforced starting W27 2025-06-29, NB spend forced down from ~$25K/wk pre-W27 to ~$8K/wk by W36 (-68% trough). H2 2025 NB CPA is policy-capped, NOT demand-revealed — excludes from demand-elasticity fits via (is_structural_baseline=FALSE, half_life_weeks=0, end_date=2026-01-01) filter semantics. Architecturally identical to an OCI launch in that it redefined the spend envelope; modeled as an exclusion window rather than a structural anchor because we want fitters to SKIP this data, not anchor on it. [ADDED 2026-04-23 to correct MPE Y2026 @ 75% projection using H2 2025 budget-constrained data as if it were demand signal.]
Polaris INTL MX launch
Semana Santa (Holy Week) — MX shuts down, ~35% volume suppression. W14 2026. Recurring annual event.

MX Sparkle campaign onset W14 2026-04-05. Brand regs stepped 210 → 367/395 at W14-W16, a ~80-90% lift. Authored half_life_weeks=26 as a medium-decay default — see session-log 2026-04-23 scenario matrix (fast/medium/slow/no-decay yielding K/K/K/.11M Y2026 @ 75%). Weekly regime-fit hook (ps.regime_fit_state) will refine peak and decay as post-onset data accumulates. Structural baseline (IS TRUE) because Sparkle is a real intervention; finite half-life because campaigns decay. Combined Polaris INTL (2025-08-28, permanent) × Sparkle (2026-04-05, decaying) gives MX its current regime stack.

MX NB CPA saturation 2026-W16: NB CPA +29% WoW ($142 -> $183) driven by CPC +10% and CVR -15% on +5% higher NB spend. Sparkle campaign lifting Brand (+400% YoY W15/W16 against a mid-April seasonal headwind that dragged 2025 Brand -11% to -14%) is absorbing a share of demand that historically converted on NB, and we have pushed NB spend into the flat part of the response curve (YoY NB clicks only +13% on much higher spend). Recommended action: hold NB spend flat two weeks to re-observe. NOT a tracking, reftag, or CVR-lag issue. Originally filed 2026-04-22 as W15 investigation based on early callout snapshot; rewritten 2026-04-23 against settled W15/W16 data. Half-life 4 weeks (saturation effect decays as Brand-cannibalization share stabilizes).

Weekly regime-fit hook (ps.regime_fit_state) will refine peak and decay as post-onset data accumulates. Structural baseline (IS TRUE) because Sparkle is a real intervention; finite half-life because campaigns decay. Combined Polaris INTL (2025-08-28, permanent) × Sparkle (2026-04-05, decaying) gives MX its current regime stack.

_spend_bounds.rationale: NB floor $15K: below this is policy pullback not demand (H2 2025 precedent). NB ceiling $30K: W16 saturation diagnosis 2026-04-23 (CPC +10% WoW, CVR -15% at $21K/wk). Brand ceiling $10K: 2x current ~$6K/wk run-rate, beyond requires Sparkle-intensity review. Added 2026-04-23 per three-mechanism override framework.

weeks.sql_or_fn: Weekly outputs from project_with_locked_ytd — Brand per-week from brand_trajectory, NB per-week from solve_nb_residual × seasonal weights

WW.banner: US CA UK IT ES MX AU mixed fallback

MX.remediation: Investigate data-quality drift in ps.v_weekly; check for recent regime onset not yet in ps.regime_changes.
